author: Sofia Martínez
title: Perplexity Is the New Google for Research Queries — Here's Your AEO Strategy
description: Perplexity now handles 10M+ daily searches and is the go-to for research-mode queries. Here's how to get cited in its answers.

sections.p: Perplexity AI quietly became the answer engine of choice for research-mode searches in 2025. Users who want a synthesized answer with cited sources — instead of a list of blue links — turn to Perplexity first. For businesses that publish educational content, this is a distribution channel most competitors haven't touched yet.

sections.h2: What Perplexity looks for in sources

sections.p: Perplexity's model is trained to prefer sources that are specific, recent, and authoritative. It heavily weights sites that are indexed on both Google and Bing, have earned backlinks from relevant domains, and publish content with explicit author attribution. Anonymous content gets deprioritized.

One underrated factor: Perplexity tracks citation frequency. If your content gets cited in its answers and users don't dispute it, the model gains confidence in your domain. Early citations beget more citations — getting in now, before your niche is crowded, is a compounding advantage.

sections.h2: The content types Perplexity cites most

list: Comparison content: 'X vs Y' and 'best X for Y scenario' articles with clear conclusions.
Step-by-step guides with numbered steps and specific tools named.
Local market data: pricing benchmarks, regional statistics, city-specific advice.
Expert opinion pieces with named author credentials.

sections.h2: AEO vs SEO — what's different in practice

sections.p: SEO asks: 'what keyword does this target?' AEO asks: 'what question does this fully answer?' The shift sounds subtle but produces very different content. AEO content gives the complete answer in one place — no need to visit five other pages. That completeness is what earns a citation.

For local service businesses, AEO means writing content that answers the complete client question: not just 'yes we do roofing' but 'here's what a roof replacement costs in your city, how long it takes, what to watch out for, and how to choose a contractor.' One thorough page beats ten thin pages every time.

sections.h2: How we build Perplexity-ready content for clients

sections.p: Every service page we build includes a structured FAQ section, explicit author credit, and a local data section with city-specific context. We also ensure every page has clear schema markup. The combination of these signals is what's getting our clients cited consistently in Perplexity answers for their service areas.

sections.quote: Perplexity doesn't rank pages. It cites sources. The difference changes everything about how you write.
